programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Joe Wilkinson and Alex Brooker take on Kevin Bridges and Judi Love. Brian Butterfield (Peter Serafinowicz) joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours in...
programme.desc: David ODoherty joins Joe Lycett for a Costa del stroll along the Caminito del Rey, a drink from a 100-year-old barrel, a spot of dolphin watching and a visit to Pablo Picassos birthplace.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Tay House in Perth and Kinross, where the BandBers arent too happy about a lack of TVs. And theres something fishy going on at breakfast.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the Aberlour Hotel in Moray, where the BandBers are in good spirits at a distillery. But theres a hangover at feedback time.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Visit three is to Cobblehouse Country Cabins in Aberdeenshire. Graham and Emma hope the BandBers will be wowed by their wood. Will everyone be left pining for more?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to Trinity Boutique BandB in Peterhead in Aberdeenshire. Host Yuliya treats her guests to an afternoon in a prison, before wowing her guests with a breakfast thats considerably more substantial than porridge.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its payment day, and the holiday hosts meet for one last time to find out how much theyve been paid and settle some scores. After cutting questions, cleanliness concerns and a Moroccan mismatch, the BandBers find out whos won the week.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: A fresh set of BandBers descend on Greenhills Hotel in Tenby, Pembrokeshire, where hosts Maxine and Tegan are hoping to make their guests feel like part of the family. After some picky room inspections, the BandBers cause a commotion on a seal safari.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Self-confessed hospitality obsessive Tom hosts at his Three Cliffs Bay Holiday Park in Gower, Swansea. Hes keen to show his guests a slice of paradise at his site, which offers both glamping yurts and caravan stays.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to The Tal-Y-Cafn in Conwy. Chef Dan and his partner Lucy hope to cook up a storm for their guests. The hosts make a splash with an afternoon of inland surfing, but Dans cockiness about his breakfast proves misplaced.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks final visit is to Cambrian House BandB in Carno, Powys, where husband-and-wife team Jacqui and Ivan think their value for money will be unbeatable. And first impressions are certainly good.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its an extra-special payment day, as `Four in a Bed celebrates its 1000th episode. To mark a millennium of sausage squabbles, decor disagreements and a feast of feedback thunderbolts, theres a savage and explosive argument between two BandBers.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Henry Cole head to Norfolk, where Simon converts a trough into a bioethanol patio heater, and Henry uncovers a vintage stationary engine.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Wiltshire, Henry Cole and Gemma Longworth restore a vintage car, a weaving loom and a dough proving bin.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Henry Cole head to Buckinghamshire, where their restorations include a pair of manhole covers, a water pump and a beehive.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Bob and Vida want to buy a holiday bolthole on the west coast of the Peloponnese that one day could become their permanent home. Can Laura Hamilton find the perfect place for their £300k?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson helps Dipti find a holiday home on Spains Costa Almería. Joining Dipti on her search in Mojácar is her best friend Anne. With a budget of £130,000, Jean shows them five stunning properties. But will any of them impress Dipti?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson is with Julie and Amanda. They want to find a holiday home where they can unwind and relax, and maybe make a permanent move to in the future! They have settled on Lot-et-Garonne. Jean lines up five options for their budget of £120,000.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Rocky the bulldog has had a much-needed facelift for his date, but only has eyes for a toy avocado. Muddy puddle enthusiast Grady the lurcher is looking for a new thrill.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Love Cheats
programme.desc: A serial fraudster impersonates a member of nobility to con his victims out of thousands.

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: Peter has a fast-growing tumour and needs throat reconstruction to save his life. Janets pancreatic cancer is wrapped around a vital vein - can the growth be removed?

programme.title: The Kid Who Would Be King
programme.desc: Old-school magic meets the modern world when young Alex stumbles upon the mythical sword Excalibur. He soon unites his friends and enemies into a band of knights who join forces with the legendary wizard Merlin. Together, they must save mankind from the wicked enchantress Morgana and her army of supernatural warriors.

programme.title: The Alamo
programme.desc: Star-studded saga of the 1836 defence of the Alamo, when a 187-strong garrison including William Travis and Jim Bowie fought to save Texas from Santa Annas Mexican army for 13 days. The rivalry of the two leaders was tempered by the arrival of Davy Crockett, who brought his Tennessee volunteers to fight alongside them.

programme.desc: Michael Palin provides a retrospective of the popular, well-loved and charismatic David Attenboroughs 50 years at the BBC.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: The story of life, from the first primitive cells to the plants and animals that now live around us. David Attenborough goes on a world journey looking at the incredible variety of living things and fossils which throw light on the ancestry of life.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A tigress is chosen to found a new dynasty. Airlifted from her home in an Indian park to life in a new reserve, Baghani the tigress must fight with leopards for territory and learn to hunt dangerous wild boar. Also released is Rajore, a young male.

programme.title: The Magic of Dance
programme.desc: In the 20th century, Sergei Diaghilev brought the Ballets Russes to live and work in Western Europe. Margot Fonteyn visits Tchaikovskys house near Moscow and talks to Marie Rambert about Diaghilev.

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: An Omnibus special, first broadcast in 1995, shortly after the death of Ginger Rogers. The film looks at how the legendary Fred and Ginger musicals were made and features anecdotes from Rogers and clips from Astaires last television interview.

programme.title: Puttin on the Ritz
programme.desc: A short compilation of dancer Fred Astaires days with the RKO movie studio.

programme.title: Dance Passion Swansea
programme.desc: Dancers of all kinds take over Swanseas streets, landmarks, parks and beaches to showcase the city, explore what home means and celebrate the life-enhancing joy of dance. There are solos, duets and ensembles. From ballet, Latin and contemporary to ballroom, breaking, traditional clog and Welsh folk dance. Locations range from Mumbles Pier and skateboard park to Swansea Bay and the dunes, beaches and bays of the Gower peninsula.

programme.title: Only Connect

programme.title: Lost Cities of The Ancients
programme.desc: This is the story of the formidable Hittites and their long-lost capital, Hattusha, which has recently been rediscovered. Buried in this lost city is one of the greatest libraries of the ancient world, and all the secrets of this mysterious civilisation were written in two codes - one a unique form of hieroglyphs with which we can recreate the lost world of the Hittites.

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew looks at the extraordinary art to come out of the dark Norwegian soul, most famous for producing The Scream by Edvard Munch.

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.sub-title: Jodrell Bank: Tuning Into the Universe
programme.desc: The team explore the remarkable story of Jodrell Bank Observatory and its towering Lovell Telescope, which scientists have used to listen to the cosmos for almost 70 years.

programme.title: British Art at War: Bomberg, Sickert and Nash
programme.sub-title: David Bomberg: Prophet in No Mans Land
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines how the art of David Bomberg was influenced by his experiences during World War One.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: Charting the decisive shift of power from countryside to the city during the 19th century. With the industrial revolution transforming the British Isles, a divide opened up between city and country, forcing artists to respond to the upheaval to lives and the landscape. Some artists attempt to capture the poverty and squalor caused by the rapid urbanisation around them.

programme.title: An Art Lovers Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke head to Lisbon, rapidly becoming one of Europes most popular tourist destinations. Winding through the citys cobbled streets, from its steep hills to the picturesque shoreline, the cultural riches they encounter reveal the citys fascinating history.

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: An Omnibus special, first broadcast in 1995, shortly after the death of Ginger Rogers. The film looks at how the legendary Fred and Ginger musicals were made and features anecdotes from Rogers and clips from Astaires last television interview.

programme.title: The Climate Question
programme.sub-title: How Do The Oceans Shape Our World?
programme.desc: The oceans cover about 70% of the planet and are crucial for life on Earth. Oceanographer Helen Czerski reveals how the seas around us regulate the climate and the weather, even in the desert.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive heads for the east coast of Barbados to check out the worlds oldest rum distillery. He explores the sugar cane fields and the factory floor before he gets the chance to have a sip of the golden produce.

programme.title: BAFTA Television Awards
programme.desc: The prestigious BAFTA Television Awards return with host Greg Davies at the helm. It has been a landmark year for storytelling, with Adolescence, A Thousand Blows and Amandaland all vying for top honours. From groundbreaking drama to the best in entertainment and factual programming, the creativity that defined the last twelve months will be celebrated. Viewers will be treated to a night of glamour, heartfelt speeches and spectacular live music as the best TV of 2026 is crowned.

programme.desc: Its Phoebe and Mikes big day, but all does not go to plan. programme.title: Friends

programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Is Late

programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Has a Baby

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Cruise and Stay; Real Cruise People
programme.desc: Your perfect cruise, found by The Real Cruise People.

programme.title: Shop: Thane Offers
programme.desc: Award-winning home and fitness products that let you do more with less.

programme.title: Cruise TV with LoveitBookit
programme.desc: A definitive one-stop shop for all things cruise, bringing you the hottest destinations and the latest deals to ensure that your next cruise ship holiday is your greatest one yet.

programme.title: Michael McIntyres The Wheel
programme.desc: Three contestants hope to answer their way to a fortune. To help them along, they have the guidance of Russell T Davies, AJ Odudu, Deborah Meaden, Bash the Entertainer, Lucy Beaumont, Harry Redknapp and William Hanson.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is thrilled when his name is used in a `Soap Opera Digest crossword puzzle. He is reluctant to let the magazine do a profile of him, but Rachel tries to persuade him to reconsider so that he can mention her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby Shower
programme.desc: Rachel panics when she realises how unprepared she is to care for an infant. She reluctantly agrees to allow her frustrating mother Sandra to move in to help her, upsetting Ross. Meanwhile, Monica tries to atone for forgetting to invite Sandra to the baby shower, and Chandler and Ross exhaust themselves by repeatedly playing a confusing game called Bamboozle to help Joey prepare to audition as a game show host.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Cooking Class
programme.desc: When Monicas cooking at the restaurant receives a harsh write-up in a newspaper, she goes to a criticism class taught by the reviewer, hoping to convince him to reconsider his opinion. Meanwhile, Rachel gets upset when a cute salesgirl flirts with Ross while he and Rachel are shopping for baby supplies, and nervous about a major job interview, Chandler receives some questionable coaching from Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Is Late
programme.desc: When Rachels pregnancy goes beyond the expected delivery date, the pain makes her increasingly irritable and willing to try anything that might speed up the babys arrival. Meanwhile, Monica and Phoebe bet on the date of the birth, while Joey can only take one guest to the premiere of his film, and chooses Chandler. Unfortunately, Chandler falls asleep during the screening.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Has a Baby
programme.desc: Rachel waits in the hospital maternity ward with an anxious Ross, but to her dismay she is put in a semi-private room and then, the heavily pregnant Janice, is allocated to share the room with her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Has a Baby
programme.desc: Rachel waits in the hospital maternity ward with an anxious Ross. Numerous other pregnant women are wheeled off to give birth while Rachels painful labour continues. Elsewhere in the hospital, Phoebe is attracted to a handsome patient, Cliff, and asks Joey to pose as Dr Drake Ramoray to interrogate Cliff about his background.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where No One Proposes
programme.desc: Due to a misunderstanding, Rachel finds herself engaged to Joey instead of Ross shortly after giving birth to her and Rosss baby girl. Meanwhile, Joey cant help taking a look as Rachel tries unsuccessfully to nurse her baby. Inspired to have a baby of their own, Monica and Chandler retire to a hospital cupboard for a little privacy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joeys futile attempts to gain Rosss forgiveness for mistakenly asking Rachel to marry him only result in emergency hospitalisation. Meanwhile, an exhausted Rachel desperately searches for a solution to end baby Emmas non-stop crying.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachels constant calls to Emmas paediatrician force him to reject her as a patient. Meanwhile, Chandler plans to move to Tulsa for work, but worries about whether or not career-minded Monica is still willing to join him, and Joey and Phoebe agree to find each other companions for a double date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe fears losing her latest prospective boyfriend Mike and her self-doubt is not helped by Ross careless comments. Meanwhile, Chandler is in Tulsa on a work assignment, and when Monica surprises him in his hotel room, she mistakenly thinks that he has a sexual fetish for sharks.

programme.title: Dr Dolittle
programme.desc: Dolittle and his pushy partner Mark Weller hope to strike a lucrative deal with a major corporation, but more and more of Dolittles time is taken up giving CPR to lab rats and talking down a suicidal tiger from jumping five storeys.

programme.title: Michael McIntyres The Wheel
programme.desc: Three contestants hope to answer their way to a fortune. To help them along, they have the guidance of Russell T Davies, AJ Odudu, Deborah Meaden, Bash the Entertainer, Lucy Beaumont, Harry Redknapp and William Hanson.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Phoebes Birthday Dinner
programme.desc: While Phoebe and Joey wait at a fancy restaurant for their friends arrival to celebrate Phoebes birthday, Rachel is delayed because she is afraid to leave Emma with a babysitter. Monica and Chandler also fail to arrive on time after having a row about Chandlers urge to resume smoking and then giving in to other urges.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Male Nanny
programme.desc: Ross mocks Rachels new male nanny, and Chandler becomes upset when Monica says that somebody from her job is the funniest guy shes ever met.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rosss Inappropriate Song
programme.desc: When Chandlers former romantic rival intends to sell his apartment, insecure Chandler persuades Joey to help him secretly inspect it and the guys are shocked to discover an old videotape featuring Monica having sex with Richard.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachels Other Sister
programme.desc: On the night before Thanksgiving, Rachels self-centred younger sister Amy ruins everyones holiday dinner with her uncensored observations. Meanwhile, Monica obsessively protects her good wedding china, and Joey forgets to join his television cast mates in the big Thanksgiving parade.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachels Phone Number
programme.desc: Rachel impulsively gives a handsome restaurant patron her telephone number, and worries that hell call when Ross is in their apartment. Meanwhile, Phoebes boyfriend Mike and Ross desperately try to find something in common to talk about when they babysit Emma, and Chandler returns from Tulsa to visit Monica, but must hide from Joey after rejecting his offer of free basketball tickets.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Christmas in Tulsa
programme.desc: Chandler is forced to remain in Tulsa over the Christmas period due to a major deadline at work, and Monica fears the worst when she finds out that he will be joined in Tulsa by a beautiful colleague Wendy. While he works, Chandler recalls some of the more embarrassing moments from previous holidays shared with his friends.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Goes Back to Work
programme.desc: When Rachel returns to her office to show off baby Emma, she is surprised to find handsome temp Gavin has been doing her job while she has been away on maternity leave. Their immediate war of wills prompts Rachel to make a rash decision.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe and Mike parent baby rats born in her cabinet, Rachel gets steamed after Monica invites a rival to her birthday bash, and Joey likes Emmas new nanny.

programme.title: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery
programme.desc: Spoof of 1960s spy films, in which cryogenically preserved secret agent Austin Powers is revived in the present day. Once synonymous with style, Powers soon realises that his vocabulary, fashion sense and attitude to women are hopelessly out of date. He enlists the help of a decidedly modern woman to help him adjust to the 1990s and vanquish his old adversary, Dr Evil.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: A compilation of the highlights from the series. Jimmy Carr oversees the disputes and deliberations, and presents team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and a multitude of celebrity guests.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Four longtime friends compete to embarrass each other with a series of hilarious dares. If they refuse, they lose. At the end, the big loser must endure an epic punishment.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain

programme.title: Spring Break Murders

programme.title: Spring Break Murders

programme.title: Spring Break Murders

programme.title: Spring Break Murders

programme.title: Cold Case Files: Murder in the Bayou

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: In 1972, the body of Jody Loomis is found in the woods. 34 years later, the killers DNA is recovered from Jodys old clothing but it isnt matched until 2018, when genetic genealogy helps point to one suspect.

programme.title: Cold Case Files

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: When a couple crashes into a creek along a rural motorway, investigators believe its a tragic accident. However, a dogged coroner and newly appointed prosecutor join forces to uncover the bizarre truth of what happened that deadly December night.

programme.title: Killer in Plain Sight
programme.desc: Police find Kathleen Lipscombs dead body curled up by the side of a road. Unravelling the mystery of how she got there will take them into a dark world of corruption, bribes and deadly ambition.

programme.title: Killer in Plain Sight
programme.desc: Lanell Barsocks best friend finds her fatally shot in her own house and tells police she saw Lanells boyfriend at the crime scene. This open-and-shut case is complicated by the fact the victim has more than one jealous partner.

programme.title: Killer in Plain Sight

programme.title: Killer in Plain Sight
programme.desc: Brian Barrett is shot dead in his car right before going to meet a woman with whom he corresponded online. To catch the killer, police must venture into the murky world of online chat rooms.

programme.title: My Strange Arrest

programme.title: My Strange Arrest

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Boston for the first of two visits to La Galleria 33, an Italian restaurant opened in 2006 by raucous sisters Rita and Lisa. The two siblings grew up working at their fathers successful restaurant nearby, but when they opened their own establishment, the customers didnt materialise. The sisters blame everything from the weather to the tablecloths for their failures.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA

Can Gordon wrangle these hapless proprietors and stubborn employees back into one productive team and save the restaurant?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Believing Moe to have the tormented soul of an artist, Lisa helps him discover his inner poet, but when success goes to his head and he abandons her, Homer vows revenge.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart becomes best friends with his former adversary Nelson, but when the bully catches him on an illicit rendezvous with Millhouse, Bart begins to fear for his safety.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A British secret agent comes to Springfield in search of a Russian spy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Moe breaks their most sacred rule, a secret society of bartenders seeks ultimate vengeance on Homer and his friends.

programme.desc: Our teams clown around at the circus in Cambodias second-largest city, Siem Reap. A fiddly floral task brings mud, sweat and tears. And one pair have a meltdown.

programme.title: Titanic

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Catherine and Richard are madly in love. Theyve been engaged for three years but cant afford to marry. Theyre given money towards their wedding, which Richard must organise alone. Can Richard organise the wedding and live up to Catherines expectations or will best man Phil lead him astray?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: The arrival of Katie and Darnells two daughters has put their wedding plans on hold. They can claim £12,000 towards their big day, but only on the condition that Darnell organises the whole wedding on his own. The couple will have no contact and three weeks apart, with Katie not knowing a single thing about the wedding until the big day.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Grooms Dance Routine
programme.desc: Ritesh and Sheena are in love but simply dont have the funds for a wedding, having ploughed all their pennies into their ice-cream business. They have been given £12,000 towards their big day, but there is a catch - not only will the wedding be arranged in just three weeks, but every single decision will be made by the groom and his bride will know nothing until the big day.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Travis Boersma, co-founder and president of Dutch Bros., the largest privately owned drive-through coffee shop chain in the USA, goes undercover posing as a reality TV show contestant who is trying to win money to open a restaurant.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: In this special episode, ex-undercover boss Eric Casaburi brings together six former participants from the series who were either `busted, `nearly busted or `self-busted while working in disguise among their own employees.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Leeds, Gemma and Ryan kick off the competition with a menu designed to show off their fun side. Day two sees Jonathan and Keith put on a classic 1970s menu, and finally, its the turn of lyric-tastic Nigel and Yvonne to try and grab the grand.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby fan Andy and dental nurse Holly bring a Mexican fiesta to Yorkshire. Mitesh and Jess serve an Indian-inspired meal to spice-averse guests. And, Paul and Mandy dish out a few surprises.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In South Wales, Red and Bozey, `Princess Trish and Alan, and Marc and Dan compete in a week that features tattoos, a musical-themed feast and nanas egg sauce.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In Beaver, Pennsylvania, where he finds a brother-and-sister team who are unable to work together in a positive manner at an Italian restaurant called Levantis. Their relationship is so fractured that they are failing to save their fathers business from being overwhelmed by competition from other local eateries. The situation has become desperate and dad Tony is now in danger of losing his life savings.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits family-owned restaurant Sams Mediterranean Kabob Room in Monrovia, California. Within minutes of his arrival, Gordon is caught in the crossfire of an argument between owner Sam, his wife and their children, all of whom are feeling the pressure of working in the restaurant seven days a week.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Long Beach, California, to save Ninos, an Italian family restaurant that was opened in 1958 and is now under the misguided leadership of the eldest son, Nino. Younger brother Michael and sister Carina point the finger of blame for the restaurants failings directly at Nino, and their 78-year-old mother Inge is desperate for Nino to turn the business around.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Norwalk, Ohio, in Americas farming heartland, for the first of two visits to the Mill Street Bistro. There he encounters Joe Nagy, one of the most pretentious restaurant owners he has ever met.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the dramatic conclusion to the Mill Street Bistro saga, Gordons disagreement with proprietor Joe Nagy erupts into one of his most intense confrontations yet.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Armando Iannucci causes outrage with an ostrich. Joanna Page and Alex Horne have an intimate conversation about guinea pigs. Joel Dommett enjoys hours of fun with pots and pans.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson and Rosie Jones take on Sean Lock and Harry Hill, as Jimmy Carr hosts with Rachel Riley. Nick Mohammed joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Bill Baileys Remarkable Guide to the Orchestra
programme.desc: Comedian and classically trained musician, Bill Bailey joins forces with the brilliantly versatile BBC Concert Orchestra and musical maestro Anne Dudley.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Theres a huge buzz in the Quinn household as preparations are made for the annual day trip to Barrys amusement park in Portrush. Surely an unfeasible amount of sandwiches and a massive surfboard is all they could possibly need.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Jamess driving skills are put to the test as the gang set off in a van full of hooch and high hopes to an adult-free house in Donegal. The Quinns visit local psychic Carlos Santini, in the hope of making contact with a much-loved family spirit.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Uncut
programme.desc: An extended version of the show in which team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock join host Jimmy Carr and guests Carol Vorderman, Chris Ramsey, Mel Giedroyc and Susan Calman.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined in their quest to uncover the publics most popular taking points by Jack Dee, Steve Jones, David ODoherty and Jamelia.

programme.desc: Linda L Chadwick, president and CEO of Ritas Italian Ice and Frozen Custard, goes under cover to see how new initiatives are working, and to help franchisees become more profitable.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Michelle and Robs starter home was perfect a decade ago, but with three kids and an in-home day-care, it is now bursting. They are more than ready to move, but first they have got to overhaul their confused floor plan and kid-centric interiors.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bedfordshire, Tom and Naomi prepare posh nosh, Victoria and Elliot serve a controversial goat curry and Neil and Charlie plan to impress their guests with mystery and magic.

CEO Andy Wirth has been busy merging Squaw Valley with newly acquired mountain resort Alpine Meadows. He dons a disguise to go undercover and find out how the combined operation is running.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Based in Los Angeles, Fatburger is known as the `last great hamburger stand. It began in the 1950s as a late-night hangout for musicians and quickly grew to international success. Andy Wiederhorn, CEO of Fatburger, disguises himself to go incognito among his employees.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Wilkinsburg, Pennsylvania, home to Ms Jeans Southern Cuisine, which was opened 12 years ago by retired special education teacher Ms Jean Gould. Her employees say shes like Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde and thats why the local community stay away. Ms Jean insists shes only mean because her employees are lazy, but the business is falling apart.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across the United States in a bid to save failing restaurants from disaster. This week, Gordon visits beachside restaurant Barefoot Bobs in Hull, Massachusetts, owned by husband-and-wife team Marc and Lisa. The duo opened the grill in the summer of 2004 to a busy seafront crowd, but every year the bottom line collapses through the bleak winter months.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Jonathan tackles a budget-friendly renovation for Jessica and Taylors home for 16 years, ahead of the large extended family all moving on in new directions.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Nottingham, three couples put their hosting skills to the test, in a week featuring belching, balloon bursting, gargling games and `slimy food.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Greg and Alex take things up a notch by introducing a pulper to proceedings. Jelly babies cause friction between the teams. And we discover that Mae Martin and Ivo Grahams go-to noun is duck.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by Freddie Flintoff and Rhod Gilbert. Henning Wehn joins Susie Dent in `Dictionary Corner, while Joe Wilkinson `helps Rachel Riley provide the letters and numbers.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by guests Josie Long and Rob Beckett. Vic Reeves joins Susie Dent in `Dictionary Corner, while Joe Wilkinson `helps Rachel Riley provide the letters and numbers.

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: A one-off special episode written and directed by Graham Linehan. The reputations of Roy, Jen and that of Reynholm Industries are at risk when an incident involving spilt coffee and a homeless person ends up on the internet.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Sketch impeaches Lewyss presidential portrait. Pash covers Rihannas regretful reminder. Alice creates an original design for Ellie, who survived an acid attack.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Greg and Alex take things up a notch by introducing a pulper to proceedings. Jelly babies cause friction between the teams. And we discover that Mae Martin and Ivo Grahams go-to noun is duck.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Boston for the first of two visits to La Galleria 33, an Italian restaurant opened in 2006 by raucous sisters Rita and Lisa. The two siblings grew up working at their fathers successful restaurant nearby, but when they opened their own establishment, the customers didnt materialise. The sisters blame everything from the weather to the tablecloths for their failures.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Leon wants to get wed on a beach in Weymouth. But whilst Leon slowly gets his wacky wedding in motion, his traditional bride Kirsty dreams of a romantic fairy-tale day at a venue that means something special to her.

programme.title: Hollyoaks: Omnibus
programme.desc: Everyone turns to Vicky for answers about the crash. Diane breaks the news of her prognosis to her family. Tensions rise between Sienna and Cleo. Warren and Jenson lock horns over Mercedes.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: This seasonal special reminds us that Christmas is about giggling as well as giving. Claire and Phil go all Grinch-y, and Lily is taken to see Santa.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The arrival of Mannys father stirs tension between Gloria and Jay, Claire has to rely on a sexy fireman in order to help Phil get to the hospital and Lily is being sleep-trained by Mitchell and Cameron.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell sows the seed that one of Jays buddies is secretly gay, and Claire wages war against a remote control.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay tries to help two inexperienced restaurant owners who run an Italian eatery called Casa Roma in Lancaster, Calif.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon confronts restaurant owners and sisters Rita and Lisa with an ultimatum. His stand-off with the siblings is just the beginning of the explosive action. He finds himself stuck in a war between the waiters, chefs and owners over who is to blame for the restaurants failings. Can Gordon wrangle these hapless proprietors and stubborn employees back into one productive team and save the restaurant?

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The couples have met their alternate matches. But now its time for the real carnage: getting them to watch videos of how their spouses behaved.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The final dinner party is upon us, and one couple are on the brink after the tumult of meeting their alternate matches.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge is cast as Blanche DuBois in a local musical production of `A Streetcar Named Desire. The show stinks but Homers crass comments inspire Marge to give the performance of a lifetime.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Mook, the Chef, the Wife and Her Homer
programme.desc: The Simpsons become immersed in Springfields criminal underworld when Homer becomes the Don of a local mob family.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Believing Moe to have the tormented soul of an artist, Lisa helps him discover his inner poet, but when success goes to his head and he abandons her, Homer vows revenge.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart becomes best friends with his former adversary Nelson, but when the bully catches him on an illicit rendezvous with Millhouse, Bart begins to fear for his safety.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A British secret agent comes to Springfield in search of a Russian spy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Moe breaks their most sacred rule, a secret society of bartenders seeks ultimate vengeance on Homer and his friends.

programme.title: The Amazing Race Australia: Celebrity Edition
programme.desc: Our teams clown around at the circus in Cambodias second-largest city, Siem Reap. A fiddly floral task brings mud, sweat and tears. And one pair have a meltdown.

programme.title: Titanic
programme.desc: A poor Irish artist and a rich debutante meet and fall in love on the famously ill-fated maiden voyage of the `unsinkable RMS Titanic in 1912.

programme.title: Copshop
programme.desc: On the run from a lethal assassin, a wily con artist devises a scheme to hide out inside a small-town police station. However, when the hitman turns up at the precinct, an unsuspecting rookie cop finds herself caught in the crosshairs.

programme.desc: Married couple Abbie and Charlie are missing one thing from their family: a woman to turn them into a throuple. And glamour puss Chrissie is looking for husband number four.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Flashy groom Kain could be shooting himself in the foot planning a gangster-themed wedding. After all, his bride-to-be Charlie has always dreamed of a wedding day fit for a princess.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Everyone flies down to Florida to be with Phil after his moms passing. Claire helps Phil with Gracies last wish for Frank, and the kids reflect on the gifts grandma left for them.

programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety.

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: In the shadow of a dark internet conspiracy theory, a group of elitists gathers at a remote location to hunt humans for sport. But their master plan is about to be derailed when one of the hunted, Crystal, turns the tables on her pursuers.

programme.title: Jingle All the Way
programme.desc: Harassed businessman Howard tells his little boy hell buy him a Turbo Man for Christmas, only to find that every store has sold out. Racing round the shops in search of the last toy, he realises that postman Myron is doing exactly the same thing.

programme.title: Tooth Fairy
programme.desc: Nicknamed the `Tooth Fairy due to his penchant for knocking out other players teeth, an arrogant hockey players moniker becomes very apt when he steals a dollar that his girlfriend Carlys daughter has left for the real Tooth Fairy. Sprouting wings, Derek is summoned to meet the leader of the fairies, Lily, who labels him a `dream crusher and orders him to serve as a tooth fairy to make up for his transgressions.

programme.title: The Art of Racing in the Rain
programme.desc: Denny Swift is a Formula One race car driver who understands that the techniques needed on the racetrack can also be used to successfully navigate everyday life. Besides his career, Denny has three loves of his life -- his beautiful wife, their young daughter and his best friend -- a dog who wants to be reincarnated into a human.

programme.title: Sink the Bismarck!
programme.desc: During World War II, the German battleship Bismarck is hunted by British naval vessels. After escaping the British naval blockade, the German ship heads towards the occupied French port of Brest, where it can enjoy relative safety with its sister ships. However, at the Admiraltys London HQ, a plan is coordinated to track and sink the terror of the seas.

programme.desc: Riley awakens from a coma, after surviving an attack that killed both her husband and her daughter. When justice fails to prevail, Riley becomes an urban guerilla to serve up her own brand of punishment.

programme.desc: CIA agent Mason "Mace" Brown joins forces with a rival German agent, a cutting-edge computer specialist and a Colombian psychologist when a top-secret weapon falls into the hands of a group of mercenaries.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam tastes some of the most delicious sausage dishes in America. Later, he attempts to eat a six-pound scramble of eggs, sausage, pepperoni, and hash browns.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam samples a colossal breakfast consisting of scrambled eggs, home fries and bacon. Later, he tries to conquer a six-pound chilli omelette.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam is in Portland, where is going to sample an elegant lobster roll sandwich. Later on, he is given the challenge of eating his way through a seven-pound seafood bounty in one hour.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam travels to Hawaii to learn an authentic family recipe for succulent Pipikaula beef ribs. Then, he takes on the daunting Eagles Deli Challenge.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: In Kansas City, Adam bites into smoky baby back ribs and competes in the Shut Up Juice Challenge. He must finish a pulled pork sandwich dripping with habanero BBQ sauce.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam digs into a one-pound Reuben sandwich and the meaty King Torta. Then, he is challenged to consume five fat sandwiches in 45 minutes.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam travels to North Carolina to try a 20-ounce bacon double-cheeseburger. Later, he is given one hour to finish off the spiciest burger in Texas.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam visits a joint in Oklahoma to try a juicy 22-ounce prime rib. Later on, he takes on the challenge of eating five burger patties topped with American cheese and tortilla chips.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam is in Detroit to sample a grilled hot dog smothered in chilli and mustard. Later, he is challenged to eat five massive hot dogs with a huge side of fries.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam shares his most overstuffed sandwich moments. He competes in Luckys Challenge: finish three massive sandwiches plus fries and coleslaw in one hour.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: Fantastic recipes for tasty sweet breads, such as chocolate and mandarin bread, and scones with jam and marmalade.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: James goes back to basics demonstrating how to make the most delicious, light sponges. Recipes include Victoria sponge, American baked cheesecake and a chocolate roulade.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: Focusing on posh puds, recipes include poached pears with cinnamon and goats milk sauce, sherry trifle with raspberries and lemon and goats cheese tart.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: James Martin is one of Britains premier pastry and pudding makers, here he presents some recipes for lemon meringue pie, millefeuille, toffee bananas, banana and custard souffle and ice cream parcels with sponge pudding.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bake Off is back. The tent awaits 12 of the nations best amateur bakers whore out to impress judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, joined by Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its week two and our remaining 11 bakers face Cake Week, kicking off with a crowd-pleasing signature challenge.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Bread Week, with the 10 bakers having much to prove with a fruity teatime Signature, a rather international-flavoured Technical, and an ambitious Showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Dessert Week and the bakers must create a cracking meringue Signature, a wobbly Technical, and a complex chocolate Showstopper that promises to reveal all.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Football
programme.desc: Adam Richman combines his two great passions, food and football. He visits Britains most famous teams and seeks out footies finest foods.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Football
programme.desc: Adam Richman is in Birmingham for an Aston Villa home game. He gets to tuck into an Indian mixed grill at a Desi Pub, tries a Champions League standard mushroom ravioli and enjoys a pad Thai at a Villa fans matchday favourite; The Barton Arms.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Atlanta is the next stop on Adams culinary tour, where he attempts to finish a 13-pound Carnivore Pizza. However, this is one challenge hell need some help with.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: The next stop on Adam Richmans culinary tour of the United States is Boston, where he encounters the giant Eagles Challenge Burger. Does it come with a giant soda?

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The time has come to cross the border from Scotland to England and where better to begin this leg of the journey than James home country of Yorkshire. Here hes joined by Michelin starred chef Lisa Allen. His first stop is Whitby where hes popping into his favourite fish and chip shop before cooking tikka mackerel buns with fennel and apple salad for the crowds at Whitby bandstand. Lisa is paying a visit to The Black Swan.

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James continues his British adventure in Lancashire with top local chef Lisa Allen in the passenger seat of his trusty red mini. Their first stop is an incredible food hall in Lisas home town of Clitheroe. Here they shop and then cook together a fantastic BBQd john dory with kohlrabi and fennel salad. Next, Lisa is in the driving seat as she takes the mini to a local buffalo farm where theyre producing some interesting cheese from buffalo milk.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri road trips across America to check out classic greasy spoons. With a new generation of owners and delicious homemade food, these are the kind of places that make America special.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris coasting through some killer cuisines! A Trinidadian shop in Marietta, GA, is plating lights-out oxtail, a Los Angeles Thai spot is serving beefed-up boat noodles, and an Italian joint near Cincinnati is firing up primo pancetta bucatini.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: The next stop on Adam Richmans culinary tour of the United States is Boston, where he encounters the giant Eagles Challenge Burger. Does it come with a giant soda?

programme.title: Nigel Slaters Dish of the Day
programme.desc: Nigel Slater shows how one can, with a bit of culinary creativity, turn the contents of his weekly shop into seven delicious dishes - one for every day of the week. Here, Nigel shows ways to cook with an abundance of ingredients, whether a glut from the garden or a special offer in the shops. He proves there are creative ways to have delicious food every day of the week.

programme.title: Nigel Slaters Dish of the Day
programme.desc: Cook Nigel Slater shows how the shrinking violets of the kitchen can help make cooking shine as he turns some often overlooked staples into sensational dishes, with a recipe for every day of the week. These include haddock chowder, crumbed lamb steak with buttered swede mash, and fennel feta linguine.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: As she prepares to open a boarding house, Ree Drummond road tests mini bar snacks on the managers. Her first treat is hometown toffee, a mash-up of candy, chocolate and trail mix.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Its a full day of working with the calves on the ranch, so Ree Drummond is grilling up a well-deserved tailgate lunch. On the menu are smokey salt and pepper drumsticks, flame-grilled cheesy steak burgers, grilled taters and blueberry pies.

programme.title: Jamies Great Britain
programme.desc: Jamies visits the West Country, famous for pasties, cheddar and cider. However, Jamies in search of less traditional fare, and has some Jamaican cooking in Bristol and Guyanese specialities with Jules Davis. Jamie forages for wild mushrooms with local chef Toby Gritten, cooks up a recipe of bath chaps (pigs cheeks), and tries some local scrumpy with the Wilkins family at their apple farm in Somerset.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Farm Stand
programme.desc: Former food store owner, business woman, cookbook writer and TV food presenter Ina Garten.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Former food store owner, business woman, cookbook writer and TV food presenter Ina Garten.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Festival Week as the bakers draw inspiration from around the world, including something deep fried and Italian, and a spectacular Indonesian celebration cake.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: Mary Berry shows us how to host a traditional tea party without any fuss. She shares a clever trick for making sandwiches in advance, she bakes bite-sized scones for a cream tea, and her granddaughters help her to make orange butterfly cakes.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined in Northern Ireland by his good pal and local boy Paul Rankin. They brave the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge together, then James cooks a tasty fish dish with the dramatic coastline as his backdrop. Theres a fishing trip over to Rathlin Island on the menu too, where James tastes some dishes made from the local kelp. James and Paul then visit the Old Lammas Fair, where they taste some of the delicious seafood.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James returns to Northern Ireland with his old chef buddy Paul Rankin, where theyre exploring the area around Lough Neagh, the biggest freshwater lake in the British Isles. James and Paul discover a wasabi farm, a rarity outside of Japan. Next, Paul visits an old friend, a local honey producer, while James cooks overlooking the dramatic backdrop of Slieve Gullion: the highest point in Armagh.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: Michel goes fishing with an old friend and makes an alfresco fish dish by the river. Plus, he makes watercress soup, a winter salad, and roast apples.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy investigates the love and attention aimed at three culinary icons beloved by their clientele.

programme.desc: Guy goes back in time this week, to visit some of the top retro-style diners.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam devours a maple doughnut smothered with bacon and competes in the kitchen sink challenge, which means finishing two gallons of ice cream with eight toppings and whipped cream.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: A showcase of some of Adams greatest pizza moments. Adam bites into a Reuben pizza covered in Swiss cheese and competes in the stuffed pizza challenge, in which he must finish 10lbs of pizza or join the wall of shame.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Peppermint and a Polar Bear Plunge
programme.desc: Buddy makes a cake for the Coney Island Polar Bear Club and is invited to take a winter swim with them. It is also his youngest sons birthday and he asks for an ice cream cake.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Tournament of Knights and a Tasty Tiramisu
programme.desc: Buddy and his team are asked to make a cake for the King of Medieval Times and Buddy is challenged to a joust. They also make an old-school tiramisu cake for an Italian customer.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a tightly fought battle today as the two remaining London and South East chefs cook all four of their dishes again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge, Joy Hunter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the Central regions top chefs, returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett go into battle. They begin with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett fight it out for fish course glory. After plating up late last time will Aktar manage to be on time today?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central region chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poynton and Jason Hodnett go head to head cooking their main courses for the veteran judge. With Mark and Aktar fighting for the lead can Jason sneak under the radar to take them by surprise?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the Central chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poyton and Jason Hodnett are all striving for one of two spots in the regional final. Will Jasons elaborate chocolate medal dessert see him triumph or sent home empty handed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central region chefs cook all four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge Max Hastings.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course and as the chefs arrive they discover theres added competition following the London and South East regions shock result. The judges are joined by D-Day veteran George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and those chefs who didnt make it onto the shortlist with their starters are hoping to do better. The judges are joined by Ken Sturdy who landed on Sword Beach on D-Day and who helped judge the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the half-way point in finals week and those chefs who have yet to get a dish onto the shortlist are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by Baroness Trumpington who worked as a cypher clerk at Bletchley Park from 1941 to 1946.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the last chance for the chefs to get their dish in contention for the final banquet and those whove failed to finish in the top three so far are going all out. Joining the judges is Celia Sandys, Winston Churchills granddaughter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the moment of truth as the victorious four must put their finest hours on a plate for those who served both on the front line and the home front, such as D-Day veterans Ken Sturdy and George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands top chefs, Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee, and Graham Campbell - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge. Having missed out last year, returning finalist Jak is determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell fight it out for the fish course. Graham is in a confident mood but returning finalist Jak and self-taught Jimmy are pushing him hard. Will the pressure prove too much for ambitious Graham?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell are battling to plate up a perfect main course for this weeks veteran chef. Self-taught Jimmy is honouring his mum with his rendition of her Chinese beef stew.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today its the Scottish chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow, and with one chef in the lead after a triumphant main, the pressures on for the other two to plate up perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: The Mistake that Killed Hitler
programme.desc: In April 1945, as Stalins Red Army approach from the east and the western Allied forces quickly approach through France and Belgium, Adolf Hitler awaits his fate in his bunker, reflecting on the mistakes that lost him the war.

programme.title: The Hitler Chronicles
programme.desc: Hitler becomes a police informer at the NSDAP and soon becomes a leader. He tries to lead a coup in 1923 but fails and ends up in jail for a year where he writes Mein Kampf.

programme.sub-title: A Party Leader and a Mob Orator

programme.title: Mein Kampf: The Secrets of Hitlers Books

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Germany took just four weeks to conquer Poland, using a new form of warfare - Blitzkrieg. Six armoured tank units known as Panzer divisions, supported by the Luftwaffe, quickly broke through Polish defences.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitlers assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The attack on Pearl Harbour brought the sleeping giant, America, into the war, and saw the emergence of the aircraft carrier as the pre-eminent weapon of the seas.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Mussolini wanted to recreate the Roman Empire, but proved to be a poor military strategist and his incompetence contributed to Hitlers eventual defeat.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: With their armies fully engaged on the battlegrounds, the Allied and Axis powers focused their efforts to develop new weapons and new strategies to deliver a knockout blow to the enemy and bring about a quick end to the conflict.

programme.title: World War II in Colour

programme.title: Truthseekers

programme.title: Truthseekers
programme.desc: With a famous reputation for mysteriously swallowing boats, ships and even planes, the Bermuda Triangle has haunted tales for generations.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the turbulent history of Carrickfergus, one of the most ancient castles in Northern Ireland and a stronghold that holds the key to understanding the tempestuous relationship between Britain and Ireland.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: This first episode takes Dan behind the battlements of Dover Castle, where he descends into an underground lair to discover how a vast network of tunnels has been used to defend both the castle and the country for centuries.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Built by William the Conqueror, the Tower of London is one of Britains most iconic landmarks. It has been a military fortress, a palace, a royal mint, a prison, a zoo and a place of execution, a silent witness to some of the most momentous events in our history. Dan Jones reveals some of its fascinating secrets and tells the stories of the traitors, rebels and royalty who met their end inside its imposing walls.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan is in the heart of the English countryside at Warwick Castle. While it appears idyllic today, for centuries it was a poisoned chalice to the Earls of Warwick who lived there and its walls echo with tales of betrayal, bloodshed and murder.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Historian Dan Jones is in the heart of Snowdonia exploring a stronghold built not only as an impenetrable fortress but also as a lavish royal palace fit for a fairytale - Caernarfon Castle.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Situated high on a crag overlooking the river Forth, Stirling Castle has guarded the gateway to the Scottish Highlands for centuries. The residence of kings, armies and felons, its battle-scarred walls have witnessed savagery and chivalry, intrigue, alchemy, adultery and murder. Location filming, stunning aerial shots and reconstructions bring to to life its turbulent history.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Its a family affair today - a trio of properties from a terraced house to an enormous former care home are bought by three different sets of families with very different timescales and budgets.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The trio look at a family home in Kent, a former bar in Llanelli in Wales and a terrace in Croydon, all sold at auction. Will any of the developers make a return on their investments?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom semi in Crewe, a top-floor flat in London and a terraced house in Oldham are sold under the hammer. All the owners are happy with the prices they paid, but will any of them be able to turn a profit at the end of the projects?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: While developers in Croydon in Surrey and Donnington in Shropshire wrestle with the question of whether to lose a bedroom each for a better placed kitchen or bathroom, two friends in Derby consider creating two more in their six-bedroom house.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a rundown cottage in Probus in Cornwall, a father buys a flat in Mitcham in Surrey as a long-term investment for his young daughter and a seasoned property developer buys a house in Tunstall in Stoke-on-Trent for her daughter.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is in Plaistow in London looking around a three-bedroom semi, Lucy is checking out a small piece of land in Crayford in Kent, and Martin meets a father and son who have bought a small house with a big garden in Wheelock in Cheshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former miners cottage in Aberaman in South Wales and semis in Adderley Green in Stoke and Streatham in South London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion look around auction buys in Birmingham, Oldham and Gravesend and discover what the new owners plan to do with their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion travel the country meeting developers hoping to make some money from their auction buys. Today they are in Stoke, Croydon and Warrington hearing tales from the auction rooms.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former doctors surgery in need of TLC is converted into a family home in Houghton-le-spring and a flat in a 1930s block of flats in Edgware and a detached house in a woodland garden in Ashford in Kent are also given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion looks round a large Victorian terrace in Plumstead, Martin is in Mickleover in Derby hearing a father and daughters plans for a flat and Lucy discovers what happened to a plot of land she saw five and a half years ago.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Theres a family home in Consett in County Durham, a rental investment in Derby and an auction buy in Beckenham in London which will be used to run a new business.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a bungalow in a stunning setting in the Lake District while Dion has a look at a flat close to Nottingham city centre. Lucy meets a woman who mistakenly thought she was buying a piece of land in Folkestone in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy looks at a flat in Kilburn in London with one of the worst cases of damp she has ever seen, while Martin and Dion are in the Midlands looking round semis with potential.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat with huge potential in Londons Earlsfield, and terraced houses in Wombourne near Wolverhampton and Ynyswen near Treorchy in Wales are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is impressed with the potential of two shops and three flats in Tonypandy in Wales while Dion looks round a two-bedroom maisonette in Dawley and Lucy meets a first time developer with ambitious plans for an end terrace in Londons Forest Gate.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-floor one-bedroom flat in Londons Stepney Green, a three-bed semi in Dartford in Kent and a one-bedroom flat on the 17th floor of a block in Salford are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s bungalow in Dereham in Norfolk, a first-floor flat in Londons East Ham and a property in Maryport in Cumbria with a hidden room are all sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans for their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a semi in Ilkeston in Derby, a flat in Streatham Hill in London and a bungalow in Somersets Portishead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a fifth-floor flat in Greater Manchester, a three bed semi in Carlisle and three commercial buildings near Stanstead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An old Derbyshire farmhouse in Wirksworth gets a stunning make over, a developer builds a pair of semis in Chatham in Kent while a mid-terraced house in Blackhall Colliery in County Durham is given a makeover ready for the rental market.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion meet father and daughter developers. An apartment in a former mill in Mow Cop on the Staffordshire /Cheshire borders, a three-bed semi in East Ham and a flat in Bournemouth are all given makeovers with the help of Dad.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple about to get married buy their first home in Newcastle, a new dad thinks developing a flat in Leyton in London will give him more time with his four-month-old son, and a former IT worker develops a property in Gainsborough in Lincolnshire.

programme.desc: A local judge discovery the body of a young woman floating in the water. The police try to find the person who brought 22-year-old Misty to her watery grave.

programme.title: Swamp Murders

programme.title: Real Time Crime
programme.desc: A shooting in Mississippi leaves a man fighting for his life, and police must use gunshot detection technology to track the shooter before he strikes again. Plus, in South Carolina, RTCC cameras capture a deadly bar brawl.

programme.title: Real Time Crime

programme.title: Evil Lives Here

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.sub-title: I Wished My Son Were Dead
programme.desc: It has been more than 25 years, yet Joyce Alexander is still terrified of her son, William Patrick Alexander; she fears he holds her responsible for his horrifying crime, and is convinced he is plotting his revenge from prison.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: In 1972, police find a young woman fatally stabbed in her room; police hunt for her killer, but he seemingly vanishes, and the case goes cold; decades later, a new investigative technique emerges, and the killers name turns up.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: Investigation Discovery and People magazine partner up to re-examine some of the most high profile crime cases in recent history, bringing tales of betrayal, buried secrets and unsung heroes.

programme.title: Forbidden: Dying for Love
programme.desc: When a young woman is found murdered, her case goes unsolved for a year. An arrest brings a new prime suspect, but a confession of forbidden love causes a rethink.

programme.title: Forbidden: Dying for Love
programme.desc: When a woman takes a job as a prison nurse, she begins an illicit affair with one of the inmates. However, when their love is discovered they take drastic action in order to stay together.

programme.title: Someone You Thought You Knew
programme.desc: When Lisa Stukey has not been seen or heard from for several weeks, Police find her decomposing body inside her home. The investigation leads to a shocking discovery!

programme.title: Mother, May I Murder?
programme.desc: Killers brutally slay a trucker in bed, and suspicion falls on his stepdaughter, but a darker truth emerges as police uncover a twisted plot involving teen accomplices, promises of cash and jet skis, and a chilling betrayal by those he trusted most.

programme.title: 999 Murderer Calling
programme.desc: David and Ann-Marie Pomphret seemed like a close and loving couple. When Ann-Marie is found battered to death, a distraught David dials 999. Forensic scientists detect microscopic blood cells on his unwashed socks and the truth is revealed.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: When two bodies turn up in a car crash, investigators uncover the grizzly truth about a seemingly perfect relationship.

programme.title: A Body in the Basement
programme.desc: A killer brutally murders well-known community activist and member of the gay community, Dennis Dolinger, in the basement of his Washington, DC, home.

programme.title: A Body in the Basement

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: With first-hand testimony from family members, police officers, witnesses and reporters, we shine a light on some of Britains darkest murder locations, examining the crimes that rocked these communities.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: `Happy Valley actress Katherine Kelly examines one of Scotlands most shocking murders, and the impact it had on the people living there.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: An account of the car bomb carried out by the IRA which killed 29 people in Omagh on 15 August 1998.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Throughout the early 90s young boys would be found dead after sniffing glue and being trapped in derelict buildings which caught fire.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: When a woman is brutally attacked while walking her dog in 1995, a local man evades justice once, but not twice, thanks to the double jeopardy law change.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: In 2015, a 69-year-old man is found stabbed in his home. CCTV at the bank reveals a woman using the victims card to withdraw cash. How does she relate to the murder?

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: The story of the murder of 72-year-old Jane Hings. 25-year-old Craig Keogh comes under suspicion when he is seen acting aggressively on the night of the murder.

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Morse and Lewis investigate a murder in a quiet country church where a vicar and his voluntary helpers are the unlikely prime suspects. Although at first baffled, Morse pursues his investigations religiously.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are stumped as to how to prevent weeds from choking the grape crop at Crickle Valley vineyard. Events turn sinister when a visiting wine critic dies in mysterious circumstances. Brothers Ben and Stuart, who run the vineyard and hotel respectively, are at odds over the future of the property. The situation goes from bad to worse when Bens girlfriend, and Stuarts employee Daisy is murdered.

programme.title: Agatha Christies Marple
programme.desc: A snow blizzard sets in and Miss Marple is forced to take refuge at Sittaford House, isolated on a bleak Devon moor. The owner of the house, Captain Trevelyan, inexplicably decides to spend the night at a local hotel where a ouija board predicts his death. He is later found dead in his room - and Miss Marples suspicions are confirmed.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate when the appearance of a headless horseman at Quitewell Hall foreshadows a number of deaths among an aristocratic family called the DeQuettevilles. Meanwhile, the family stage a Civil War re-enactment with their hostile neighbours and relive historic battles. Can the detectives uncover the truth about the DeQuettevilles and the ghostly rider in order to prevent more murders?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When convicted murderer Grady Felton returns to the village where he killed a boy years earlier, his arrival is met with anger from the locals. Soon afterwards, a barrister is killed in Midsomer and suspicion falls on Grady. Grady has a solid alibi, but Barnaby suspects that he is working with an accomplice - until Grady himself is targeted in an arson attack.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: As darkness covers Midsomer Stanton during a total eclipse of the sun, amateur astronomer Jeremy Harper is killed by a blow to the head with a meteorite. Barnaby and Jones soon discover intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the star-seeking community.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomers own Stella Harris. When Stellas more famous sister returns after a 40-year family rift, the killings escalate, each of them reminiscent of a movie plot, is life - and death - imitating art?

programme.title: Underwater Grand Fishing Adventure
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi is joined by Bobby Zamora and diver Luc Boerrigter as they return to The Lake of Dreams, aka Grenville. Having failed to land one of the lakes giants in front of the underwater cameras on their previous trip here together, this time they target a huge carp of over 60lbs. Being spring, the carp are constantly on the move around the lake, with the team in hot pursuit. Will the carp run rings around the team, or will the mobile tactics pay off to bank that big one?

programme.desc: Sequel unfolding a life-and-death battle to avert a nuclear holocaust. A supercomputer that sought to destroy humankind sends a virtually indestructible, shape-shifting cyborg back to our time to kill its enemy - the human resistance leader - in his youth. programme.title: Mashatu: Land of Leopards

But when Mum is injured Pemba is forced to grow up fast in order to survive.

programme.title: Ten Deadliest Snakes

programme.title: Snakes in the City
programme.desc: Simon and Sioux try to secure a black mamba escape artist, wrangle an irate python, and catch a cat killer.

programme.title: Lawless Island

programme.title: Lawless Island

programme.title: Jordan Makin: The BFFG (The Big Fat Funny Ginger)

programme.desc: Comedian Heath Harmison does celebrity impersonations,sings, dances and does accents.

programme.title: David Crowe: White Bread

programme.title: Warren B. Hall: Mostly Kid Stuff
programme.desc: Simply put, Warren B Hall is not what the viewer is expecting. He purposely avoids using the white vs black angle done by many mainstream black comedians.

programme.title: Sam Adams: Incoherently True

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mom, Dad, Peppa and George decide to go to the beach on a beautiful sunny day. Suddenly, a little cloud covers the sun for a minute, but then it passes and the sun reappears.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is on a play date with Candy Cat at Mr Cats Cafe. Candy show Mr Cat the drawings theyve done of everyone, and he hangs them up for everyone to see.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: On a cold day, Daddy Pig suggests they build a den until Mummy Pig has finished work. After a hard days work, the cushion den is a perfect place to watch TV together!
