programme.title: Thailand: Earths Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Southern Thailand is the Thailand we think we all know. It is a place of both spectacular natural beauty and of wild parties, but behind this well-known image is also a place of unexpected surprise, where spirituality pervades every bit of life. For the animals that live here, this is natural wonderland.

programme.title: Thailand: Earths Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: In central Thailands forests, fertile plains and even city streets, nature finds a way of living alongside people. Spirituality can be found in human and animal relationships, both likely and unlikely. This bustling region is known as the nations rice bowl, but even here, there are magical places to be found.

programme.title: Thailand: Earths Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Northern Thailand is dominated by mountains and cloaked in forest. It hides ancient creatures and surprising partnerships. To survive here both the wildlife and people rely on maintaining the natural harmony of the mysterious north.

programme.title: Patagonia: Earths Secret Paradise
programme.desc: Discover the secret lives of pumas and hummingbirds. Soar with condors over glacial peaks and explore monkey puzzle forests from the time of dinosaurs. Ride with extreme kayakers over raging waterfalls, and with Patagonias cowboys the gauchos, as they round up wild horses.

programme.desc: Northern Thailand is dominated by mountains and cloaked in forest. It hides ancient creatures and surprising partnerships. To survive here both the wildlife and people rely on maintaining the natural harmony of the mysterious north.

programme.title: Patagonia: Earths Secret Paradise
programme.desc: Discover the secret lives of pumas and hummingbirds. Soar with condors over glacial peaks and explore monkey puzzle forests from the time of dinosaurs. Ride with extreme kayakers over raging waterfalls, and with Patagonias cowboys the gauchos, as they round up wild horses.

programme.title: Thailand: Earths Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Southern Thailand is the Thailand we think we all know. It is a place of both spectacular natural beauty and of wild parties, but behind this well-known image is also a place of unexpected surprise, where spirituality pervades every bit of life. For the animals that live here, this is natural wonderland.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben becomes incredibly angry when he discovers that Janey is seeing another dentist, the Australian Mel Fraser. Meanwhile, Nick meets a French swimwear model on the Internet and Michael rebels in an odd way.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Michael appears to be having trouble with a classmate. Will confronting the problem make things any better? Elsewhere, Nick is trying to become a dotcom millionaire and Janey wants a tattoo.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When Janey is set up on a date with the son of one of Bens patients, things go horribly wrong. Also, Susan gets tickets for the opera but Ben is less than enthusiastic.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben decides to take Susan back to one of their old romantic haunts for her birthday, but instead, romance comes back to haunt them. Meanwhile, at home, Michael is left alone by his siblings.

programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick treats police dog Trigger, who has crushed sciatic nerves, feisty kitten Fury, who has a tibia injury, and shepherd doodle Raven, who has double hip dysplasia.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats one-year-old bouncy golden retriever Bertie who has a dislocated hip, Tibetan terrier Sunny whos almost immobile, and springer spaniel Edith who has a bulging spinal disc.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.sub-title: The Supervet at Christmas 2020: Domino, Diamond, Willow and Luigi
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team tries to get pop star Will Youngs two rescue dogs, and supermodel Yasmin Le Bons French bulldog and pug, well and home in time for the festivities.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats Caucasian shepherd dog Odin, who has wobblers syndrome. Toy poodle Monty needs a total hip replacement. And can Noel get malamute husky cross Maya back on her feet?

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The South Central crews help a boy who has dislocated his knee during a school rugby match and a man suffering with agonising stomach pains after eating a bacon sandwich.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews help a family whose car has crashed into a ditch, a woman with fast and erratic breathing, and a partially paralysed woman whos suffered a head injury in her garden.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: A girl has an operation to have squints in both eyes realigned, a man has his infected gall bladder removed and an ex-police officer is having a grommet inserted in her ear.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: A woman has an operation to repair the hernia she suffered while giving birth and a four-year-olds ear blockage is so bad he needs a general anaesthetic to have it sucked out.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Gingers for Justice take to the rooftops, The Aga Saga woman has a near meltdown when a man from the garage comes with a hired car, and the hit show `24 is spoofed.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Nan appears on `The Paul OGrady Show, The Aga Saga woman takes a wrong turn with `devastating results, and Lauren isnt bothered by her French class.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Nans sister comes over from Spain, Derek is offered a `happy ending at a massage parlour and is offended, and Lauren gets hitched.

programme.title: Death at the Mansion: Rebecca Zahau
programme.desc: A team of experts continue their in-depth search for the truth behind Rebecca Zahaus suspicious death, as they question the people who were around her when she died.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon and her gang - organizer Dilly, carpenter Rob and cleaner Iwan - challenge the Harris-Hawley family to sort their lives out in seven days. They ask the family to try to let go of half their possessions so that the team can then beautifully reorganise their home. Stacey gets to know the family behind the clutter - childhood sweethearts Dan and Char and their two daughters, Mia (11) and Cece (3) - who rent a two-up, two-down from their local council.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...
programme.desc: Louis meets Oscar-winning actress Dame Judi Dench. Having graced the stage and screen for over 60 years, including iconic turns as Queen Elizabeth in Shakespeare in Love and MI5 chief M in Bond, Judi is regarded as one of Britains greatest ever actors. Louis meets Judi at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre and her life away from acting.

programme.desc: Nan appears on `The Paul OGrady Show, The Aga Saga woman takes a wrong turn with `devastating results, and Lauren isnt bothered by her French class.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Nans sister comes over from Spain, Derek is offered a `happy ending at a massage parlour and is offended, and Lauren gets hitched.

programme.title: Death at the Mansion: Rebecca Zahau
programme.desc: A team of experts continue their in-depth search for the truth behind Rebecca Zahaus suspicious death, as they question the people who were around her when she died.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon and her gang - organizer Dilly, carpenter Rob and cleaner Iwan - challenge the Harris-Hawley family to sort their lives out in seven days. They ask the family to try to let go of half their possessions so that the team can then beautifully reorganise their home. Stacey gets to know the family behind the clutter - childhood sweethearts Dan and Char and their two daughters, Mia (11) and Cece (3) - who rent a two-up, two-down from their local council.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...
programme.desc: Louis meets Oscar-winning actress Dame Judi Dench. Having graced the stage and screen for over 60 years, including iconic turns as Queen Elizabeth in Shakespeare in Love and MI5 chief M in Bond, Judi is regarded as one of Britains greatest ever actors. Louis meets Judi at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre and her life away from acting.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Veterinarian Noel Fitzpatrick is one of Englands top animal doctors and the subject of 2010 documentary programme `The Bionic Vet, which showcased some of the work done at his Surrey clinic. On `The Supervet Fitzpatrick and his staff treat some of the countrys hardest-to-cure pets with cutting-edge treatments and surgery. The programme tells the emotional stories of the pets, their owners and the passionate team that push the boundaries of veterinary medicine to save the animals from life-threatening conditions.

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Gadsden Correctional Facility near Tallahassee in Florida where he meets Ashley Humphrey, who pumped eight bullets into a woman she had never met.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out

programme.desc: The teams first purchase is a 1972 VW Fastback, and the second project sees them pick up a 40-year-old police bike.

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Hear about the effect of World War I on the domestic front.

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: Fighting Back by Sea and Air
programme.desc: Britain needed to produce vast amounts of ammunition. Chatham Docks on the Medway saw 142 ships set sail, and bomber bases sprung up across the country.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce returns to visit Hertford College in Oxford, where she studied. Objects brought before the experts in the quadrangle include a unique record of a Beatles recording session, a Maori carving with a macabre past life, and a Russian painting which provides a shock valuation for its owner.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Dumfries House near Ayr in Scotland. Objects intriguing the experts include an early toy train in remarkable condition, an outstanding example of Chippendale furniture, and a glass vase bought at a boot sale, which hides a stunning valuation. There is also a special interview with HRH The Prince of Wales about his involvement in the rescue mission to save the unique collection.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare Honda S800 sports car from 1967 takes Dereks fancy, and a couple of very different Ford Capris with long histories go head-to-head at the auction.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons sell a 1958 Bedford D122 and a 1969 Vanden Plas Princess. They also meet a collector who may be willing to finally part with his huge hot hatch collection.

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Hear about the effect of World War I on the domestic front.

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: Fighting Back by Sea and Air
programme.desc: Britain needed to produce vast amounts of ammunition. Chatham Docks on the Medway saw 142 ships set sail, and bomber bases sprung up across the country.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: Asia is the most mountainous continent on earth. From the tropical south to its barren centre, its highest places are home to some of our planets most remarkable wildlife.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: In the remote reaches of Asias frozen north, creatures must overcome unique challenges, finding exceptional ways to thrive in a hostile world that pushes life to its very limits.

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From tropical rainforest to the Great Barrier Reef, Michael Portillo navigates his way by rail through Queensland, Australia, with the aid of his 1913 Bradshaws Guide. Inching his way across the Stony Creek Bridge on the Kuranda Scenic Railway, he marvels at the engineering expertise of the 1890s, when the line was built, and the magnificent scenery 300 metres below him.

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is on the eastern coast of Australia travelling from the coal mines of Newcastle, New South Wales, to Queenslands modern state capital, Brisbane. Along the way, he crosses the mighty Hawkesbury River to admire magnificent scenery, tends to an injured koala, gets some tips from an Aussie rugby legend and rides a solar-powered train.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Antarctica, a land of survivors enduring the most hostile conditions on Earth. Ninety eight per cent of the mainland is covered by ice on which virtually nothing can live. Even the sea freezes over, but the Weddell seal manages to survive here by keeping its breathing holes open by using its teeth to grind away the ice.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Asia is the largest and most extreme continent on our planet, stretching from the Arctic Circle in the north to the tropical forests on the equator. The animals here face the hottest deserts, tallest jungles and highest mountains found anywhere on Earth. But the continent has not always looked like this. These extreme worlds were created when India collided with the rest of Asia 30 million years ago, shaping the continent as we know it today.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will want an Eastern Bloc car to see whether the old Skoda jokes were deserved. Bidding low they win an orange Estelle - but can they add value?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien find, fix and flog unwanted possessions. Todays restorations include an old cheese press and a 1980s motorbike which almost gets the better of Henry and Guy.

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: Its the last trip before Christmas and, with demand surging, fish prices are rising. Off the south coast of Cornwall, Ocean Pride is heading out in pursuit of pollock among the old sunken shipwrecks of the English Channel. 380 miles away on the Yorkshire coast, crabber Tydus is preparing to head out with hopes of a big pay day. The aim is to put six tons in the tank, a catch that this week could fetch well over £30,000.
